nodes.description: Regular cardiac monitoring with echocardiography is recommended given the 28% prevalence of cardiomyopathy. European metabolic centers include cardiac monitoring as a consensus practice in long-term follow-up of LCFAOD patients.

evidence.reference_title: Long-term monitoring of fatty acid oxidation defects: results from a MetabERN survey.
evidence.snippet:
The centres concurred in many aspects of 
long-term monitoring of LCFAOD including the frequency of clinical visits, 
determination of laboratory parameters, cardiac monitoring and retinopathy 
screening.

evidence.reference_title: Mitochondrial bioenergetics and cardiolipin remodeling abnormalities in mitochondrial trifunctional protein deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Although CL reduction was 
universally identified, a simultaneous increase in monolysocardiolipins was 
discrepant among cells. A similar profile was seen in liver mitochondria 
isolates from a TFP-deficient mouse model.

readouts.interpretation: Reduced cardiolipin reports the TFP/HADHA-associated mitochondrial inner-membrane remodeling defect.
Lower cardiolipin is associated with abnormal mitochondrial bioenergetic parameters in TFP-deficient cells.

nodes.description: The TFP alpha-subunit (HADHA) possesses monolysocardiolipin acyltransferase activity linking fatty acid oxidation to cardiolipin remodeling. HADHA/TFP mutations disrupt cardiolipin content and composition, creating a mitochondrial inner-membrane lipid abnormality distinct from the primary beta-oxidation block.

evidence.reference_title: Mitochondrial bioenergetics and cardiolipin remodeling abnormalities in mitochondrial trifunctional protein deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Although CL reduction was 
universally identified, a simultaneous increase in monolysocardiolipins was 
discrepant among cells. A similar profile was seen in liver mitochondria 
isolates from a TFP-deficient mouse model.

evidence.reference_title: Mitochondrial bioenergetics and cardiolipin remodeling abnormalities in mitochondrial trifunctional protein deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
TFP 
also catalyzes a step in the remodeling of cardiolipin (CL), a phospholipid 
critical to mitochondrial membrane stability and function.

nodes.description: Cardiac involvement with cardiomyopathy, particularly dilated cardiomyopathy, occurring in approximately 28% of screened individuals. Reflects cardiac dependence on fatty acid oxidation for energy.

evidence.reference_title: Neurological outcome in long-chain hydroxy fatty acid oxidation disorders.
evidence.snippet:
neonatal decompensations (28%), 
symptomatic disease course (94%), later metabolic decompensations (80%), 
cardiomyopathy (28%), myopathy (82%)

evidence.reference_title: A G1528C Hadha knock-in mouse model recapitulates aspects of human clinical phenotypes for long-chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
LCHADD mice exhibit lower ketones with fasting, exhaust earlier during 
treadmill exercise and develop a dilated cardiomyopathy compared to WT mice.

nodes.description: L-carnitine supplementation to address secondary carnitine deficiency and support excretion of toxic acyl-CoA intermediates as acylcarnitines. Use remains somewhat controversial but commonly employed.

evidence.reference_title: Nutritional Management of Patients with Fatty Acid Oxidation Disorders.
evidence.snippet:
The use of carnitine remains controversial and new 
therapeutic options are under investigation.

evidence.reference_title: Long-chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Like 
several beta-oxidation defects, it presents during infancy with hypoglycemic 
coma, hepatic steatosis, and hypocarnitinemia.

nodes.description: Fasting, febrile illness, exercise, and other physiologic stressors increase dependence on long-chain fatty acid oxidation. Because LCHADD cannot efficiently use long-chain fatty acids for energy, catabolic stress produces acute energy crises with hypoketotic hypoglycemia, hepatic dysfunction, cardiomyopathy, exercise intolerance, and rhabdomyolysis.

evidence.reference_title: Long-chain fatty acid oxidation disorders and current management strategies.
evidence.snippet:
Patients may present with 
rhabdomyolysis induced by exercise; fasting or illness; hepatic dysfunction, 
including severe hypoglycemia and hyperammonemia; and cardiomyopathy.

nodes.description: Progressive chorioretinopathy unique to LCHADD and not observed in other fatty acid oxidation disorders. Involves choroidal atrophy, outer retinal layer disruption, and pigment dispersion. Observed in 17% of screened individuals and linked to RPE dysfunction and lipid peroxidation.

evidence.reference_title: Ophthalmic Symptoms of Long-Chain 3-Hydroxyacyl-CoA Dehydrogenase Deficiency: A Report of Three Cases.
evidence.snippet:
The main ophthalmic symptoms of LCHAD deficiency were choroidal 
atrophy, disorganization of the outer retinal layer, and myopia.

evidence.reference_title: The LCHADD Mouse Model Recapitulates Early-Stage Chorioretinopathy in LCHADD Patients.
evidence.snippet:
LCHADD mice have progressively decreased visual performance and 
increased RPE degeneration starting at 6 months.

nodes.description: Core dietary management involves restriction of long-chain triglycerides (LCT) to approximately 10% of total energy with supplementation of medium-chain triglycerides (MCT) at 10-25% of total energy. Essential fatty acid intake (linoleic and linolenic acid) must be maintained. Avoidance of prolonged fasting is critical.
nodes.id: Dietary fat restriction with MCT supplementation

evidence.reference_title: Nutritional Management of Patients with Fatty Acid Oxidation Disorders.
evidence.snippet:
In long-chain 
deficits, long-chain triglyceride restriction should be 10% of total energy, 
with linoleic acid and linolenic acid intake of 3-4% and 0.5-1% (5/1-10/1 
ratio), with medium-chain triglyceride supplementation at 10-25% of total energy

evidence.reference_title: Nutritional Management of Patients with Fatty Acid Oxidation Disorders.
evidence.snippet:
Treatment of fatty acid oxidation disorders is based on dietary, pharmacological 
and metabolic decompensation measures. It is essential to provide the patient 
with sufficient glucose to prevent lipolysis and to avoid the use of fatty acids 
as fuel as far as possible.

nodes.description: Acute metabolic decompensation requires emergency IV glucose administration (10% dextrose at approximately 8 mg/kg/min) to suppress lipolysis and catabolism. Cessation of protein and fat intake and monitoring of electrolytes and creatine kinase are essential.
nodes.id: Emergency glucose infusion for acute decompensation

evidence.reference_title: Long-chain fatty acid oxidation disorders and current management strategies.
evidence.snippet:
Long-chain fatty acid oxidation disorders (LC-FAODs) are rare, life-threatening, 
autosomal recessive genetic disorders characterized by acute crises of energy 
production and chronic energy deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Nutritional Management of Patients with Fatty Acid Oxidation Disorders.
evidence.snippet:
The main measure in emergency hospital treatment is the 
administration of IV glucose.

nodes.description: Reduced exercise capacity due to impaired muscle fatty acid oxidation and energy generation.

evidence.reference_title: Long-chain fatty acid oxidation disorders and current management strategies.
evidence.snippet:
Patients may present with 
rhabdomyolysis induced by exercise; fasting or illness

evidence.reference_title: A G1528C Hadha knock-in mouse model recapitulates aspects of human clinical phenotypes for long-chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
LCHADD mice exhibit lower ketones with fasting, exhaust earlier during 
treadmill exercise and develop a dilated cardiomyopathy compared to WT mice.

evidence.reference_title: Long-chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Like 
several beta-oxidation defects, it presents during infancy with hypoglycemic 
coma, hepatic steatosis, and hypocarnitinemia.

readouts.interpretation: Lower free carnitine reflects secondary carnitine depletion as accumulated long-chain intermediates are converted to acylcarnitines.
readouts.target: Toxic intermediate accumulation and oxidative stress

readouts.interpretation: Lower C0 directly tracks the clinical biochemical phenotype of hypocarnitinemia.

nodes.description: The most common pathogenic variant in LCHADD, a founder mutation affecting the LCHAD catalytic site. This variant selectively impairs LCHAD activity while relatively preserving the other two TFP enzymatic functions (enoyl-CoA hydratase and 3-ketoacyl-CoA thiolase). Carrier frequency in Finland is 1:240.

evidence.reference_title: Long-chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
In the vast majority of patients, LCHAD 
deficiency is caused by a common autosomal recessive mutation G1528C.

evidence.reference_title: Long-chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Analysis of the frequency of the G1528C mutation in Finland revealed carrier 
frequency of 1:240.

nodes.description: Biallelic pathogenic variants in HADHA reduce long-chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase catalytic activity within mitochondrial trifunctional protein.
nodes.id: HADHA molecular function deficiency within mitochondrial trifunctional protein

evidence.reference_title: Long-chain fatty acid oxidation disorders and current management strategies.
evidence.snippet:
LC-FAODs are caused by mutations in nuclear genes encoding mitochondrial 
enzymes involved in the conversion of dietary long-chain fatty acids (LCFAs) 
into energy during times of fasting and physiologic stress.

evidence.reference_title: Neurological outcome in long-chain hydroxy fatty acid oxidation disorders.
evidence.snippet:
Disease courses in screened individuals with LCHAD and MTP 
deficiency were similar except for neuropathy, occurring earlier in individuals 
with MTP deficiency (median 3.9 vs. 11.4 years; p = 0.0447).

evidence.reference_title: Mitochondrial bioenergetics and cardiolipin remodeling abnormalities in mitochondrial trifunctional protein deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
A single common mutation, HADHA c.1528G>C 
(p.E510Q), leads to isolated 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: HADHA / long chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: HADHA | HGNC:4801 | long chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency | MONDO:0012173 | AR | Definitive

evidence.reference_title: New Acylcarnitine Ratio as a Reliable Indicator of Long-Chain 3-Hydroxyacyl-CoA Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
As VLCAD-deficient patients did not show 
increased "HADHA ratio", the results emphasized the high specificity of this new 
ratio.

readouts.interpretation: The composite HADHA ratio reports elevation of LCHADD-associated 3-hydroxyacylcarnitines relative to free carnitine.
readouts.target: Impaired mitochondrial long-chain fatty acid beta-oxidation

nodes.description: Hepatic dysfunction including hepatic steatosis during metabolic decompensation, occurring in approximately 32% of screened individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Long-chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Like 
several beta-oxidation defects, it presents during infancy with hypoglycemic 
coma, hepatic steatosis, and hypocarnitinemia.

nodes.description: Secondary carnitine deficiency due to increased carnitine conjugation and excretion of accumulated long-chain intermediates.

evidence.reference_title: Long-chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Like 
several beta-oxidation defects, it presents during infancy with hypoglycemic 
coma, hepatic steatosis, and hypocarnitinemia.

nodes.description: Fasting intolerance with low ketone production and hypoglycemia, reflecting impaired fatty acid oxidation and ketogenesis during catabolic states.

evidence.reference_title: Long-chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Like 
several beta-oxidation defects, it presents during infancy with hypoglycemic 
coma, hepatic steatosis, and hypocarnitinemia.

evidence.reference_title: A G1528C Hadha knock-in mouse model recapitulates aspects of human clinical phenotypes for long-chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
LCHADD mice exhibit lower ketones with fasting, exhaust earlier during 
treadmill exercise and develop a dilated cardiomyopathy compared to WT mice.

nodes.description: Deficiency of LCHAD catalytic activity blocks the third step of long-chain fatty acid beta-oxidation, reducing acetyl-CoA and reducing equivalent supply for ketogenesis and oxidative phosphorylation. This causes energy failure during fasting, illness, or prolonged exercise when tissues depend on fatty acid oxidation for fuel.
nodes.id: Impaired mitochondrial long-chain fatty acid beta-oxidation

evidence.reference_title: Long-chain fatty acid oxidation disorders and current management strategies.
evidence.snippet:
LC-FAODs are caused by mutations in nuclear genes encoding mitochondrial 
enzymes involved in the conversion of dietary long-chain fatty acids (LCFAs) 
into energy during times of fasting and physiologic stress.

evidence.reference_title: iPSC-Derived LCHADD Retinal Pigment Epithelial Cells Are Susceptible to Lipid Peroxidation and Rescued by Transfection of a Wildtype AAV-HADHA Vector.
evidence.snippet:
LCHADD-RPE express TFP subunits and accumulate 
3-hydroxy-acylcarnitines, cannot oxidize palmitate, and release fewer ketones 
than WT-RPE.

evidence.reference_title: A G1528C Hadha knock-in mouse model recapitulates aspects of human clinical phenotypes for long-chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: LCHADD mice exhibit lower ketones with fasting

readouts.interpretation: Lower ketone production during fasting reports impaired fatty acid oxidation-driven ketogenesis.
readouts.target: Impaired mitochondrial long-chain fatty acid beta-oxidation

readouts.interpretation: Lower ketones during fasting or illness track inadequate alternative-fuel generation during catabolic stress.

nodes.description: Decreased alertness and lethargy during episodes of acute metabolic decompensation.

evidence.reference_title: Long-chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Like 
several beta-oxidation defects, it presents during infancy with hypoglycemic 
coma, hepatic steatosis, and hypocarnitinemia.

evidence.reference_title: New Acylcarnitine Ratio as a Reliable Indicator of Long-Chain 3-Hydroxyacyl-CoA Dehydrogenase Deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
The established "HADHA ratio" = (C16OH + 
C18OH + C18:1OH)/C0 was significantly elevated in all 54 affected individuals in 
comparison to the control group.

evidence.reference_title: Long-chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Diagnosis is suggested by 3-hydroxylated acylcarnitine species in blood and the 
definitive diagnosis can be made by measuring intermediates of fatty acid 
beta-oxidation in fibroblasts or by detecting disease causing mutations.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated C16OH, C18OH, and C18:1OH report the blocked long-chain LCHAD-dependent beta-oxidation step.
readouts.target: Impaired mitochondrial long-chain fatty acid beta-oxidation

readouts.interpretation: Elevated long-chain 3-hydroxyacylcarnitines report the accumulated toxic intermediary pool.
readouts.target: Toxic intermediate accumulation and oxidative stress

nodes.description: Recurrent episodes of metabolic decompensation triggered by fasting, febrile illness, or physiologic stress, occurring in 80% of screened individuals. Presents with lethargy, vomiting, and hypoglycemia.

evidence.reference_title: Long-chain fatty acid oxidation disorders and current management strategies.
evidence.snippet:
Despite newborn 
screening, current management options leave many patients continuing to 
experience major clinical events, and mortality rates remain elevated.

nodes.description: TFP-deficient patient fibroblasts show altered mitochondrial bioenergetic parameters downstream of cardiolipin and phospholipid remodeling abnormalities. This separates the respiratory/bioenergetic consequence from the upstream cardiolipin remodeling defect.

evidence.reference_title: Mitochondrial bioenergetics and cardiolipin remodeling abnormalities in mitochondrial trifunctional protein deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Abnormalities in these parameters varied 
extensively among different fibroblasts, and some cells were able to maintain 
basal oxygen consumption rates similar to controls.

nodes.description: Skeletal myopathy is the most common long-term complication, occurring in 82% of screened individuals. Manifests as exercise intolerance and recurrent rhabdomyolysis triggered by fasting, illness, or exertion.

evidence.reference_title: Neurological outcome in long-chain hydroxy fatty acid oxidation disorders.
evidence.snippet:
neonatal decompensations (28%), 
symptomatic disease course (94%), later metabolic decompensations (80%), 
cardiomyopathy (28%), myopathy (82%)

nodes.description: Progressive shortsightedness observed in LCHADD patients as part of the ophthalmic spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Ophthalmic Symptoms of Long-Chain 3-Hydroxyacyl-CoA Dehydrogenase Deficiency: A Report of Three Cases.
evidence.snippet:
The main ophthalmic symptoms of LCHAD deficiency were choroidal 
atrophy, disorganization of the outer retinal layer, and myopia.

nodes.description: Regular retinal screening for chorioretinopathy is essential for all LCHADD patients, as choroidal atrophy and pigment dispersion are early signs of retinal degeneration. European metabolic centers concur on the importance of retinopathy screening as part of long-term monitoring.

evidence.reference_title: Long-term monitoring of fatty acid oxidation defects: results from a MetabERN survey.
evidence.snippet:
The centres concurred in many aspects of 
long-term monitoring of LCFAOD including the frequency of clinical visits, 
determination of laboratory parameters, cardiac monitoring and retinopathy 
screening.

evidence.reference_title: Ophthalmic Symptoms of Long-Chain 3-Hydroxyacyl-CoA Dehydrogenase Deficiency: A Report of Three Cases.
evidence.snippet:
Choroidal 
atrophy and pigment dispersion were consistently the earliest signs of 
LCHAD-associated chorioretinopathy.

nodes.description: Chronic peripheral neuropathy is a recognized long-term complication, occurring in 22% of screened individuals. Onset is later in isolated LCHADD (median 11.4 years) compared to combined MTP deficiency (median 3.9 years).

evidence.reference_title: Long-chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
In addition, LCHAD deficiency has specific features, 
namely peripheral neuropathy and chorioretinopathy.

nodes.description: The retinal pigment epithelium (RPE) relies on fatty acid oxidation for energy. In LCHADD, RPE cells accumulate 3-hydroxyacylcarnitines and are susceptible to DHA-triggered lipid peroxidation, leading to RPE degeneration and vision loss. Exogenous HADHA gene addition rescues the biochemical and oxidative stress phenotypes.

evidence.reference_title: iPSC-Derived LCHADD Retinal Pigment Epithelial Cells Are Susceptible to Lipid Peroxidation and Rescued by Transfection of a Wildtype AAV-HADHA Vector.
evidence.snippet:
LCHADD-RPE express TFP subunits and accumulate 
3-hydroxy-acylcarnitines, cannot oxidize palmitate, and release fewer ketones 
than WT-RPE.

evidence.reference_title: The LCHADD Mouse Model Recapitulates Early-Stage Chorioretinopathy in LCHADD Patients.
evidence.snippet:
LCHADD RPE/sclera samples had a 5- to 7-fold increase in long-chain 
hydroxyacylcarnitines compared to WT, suggesting an impaired LCHAD step in 
long-chain FAO.

nodes.description: Stress-induced skeletal muscle breakdown with elevated creatine kinase, triggered by fasting, febrile illness, or prolonged exercise.

evidence.reference_title: Long-chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Other manifestations are 
cardiomyopathy and rhabdomyolysis, which are frequent in defects of long-chain 
fatty acid oxidation.

evidence.reference_title: Long-chain fatty acid oxidation disorders and current management strategies.
evidence.snippet:
Patients may present with 
rhabdomyolysis induced by exercise; fasting or illness

nodes.description: Impaired LCHAD activity leads to accumulation of long-chain 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA intermediates and their corresponding acylcarnitines (C16OH, C18OH, C18:1OH). These toxic intermediates induce oxidative stress, lipid peroxidation, and altered cell homeostasis, contributing to tissue damage in heart, skeletal muscle, liver, and retina.
nodes.id: Toxic intermediate accumulation and oxidative stress

evidence.reference_title: iPSC-Derived LCHADD Retinal Pigment Epithelial Cells Are Susceptible to Lipid Peroxidation and Rescued by Transfection of a Wildtype AAV-HADHA Vector.
evidence.snippet:
When LCHADD-RPE are exposed to docosahexaenoic acid (DHA), they 
have increased oxidative stress, lipid peroxidation, decreased viability, and 
are rescued by antioxidant agents potentially explaining the pathologic 
mechanism of RPE loss in LCHADD.

nodes.description: Triheptanoin is an FDA-approved odd-chain triglyceride providing anaplerotic substrate (propionyl-CoA converted to succinyl-CoA) to support TCA cycle intermediate replenishment and energy generation in long-chain fatty acid oxidation disorders. Reported to reduce decompensation frequency and hospitalizations.

evidence.reference_title: Long-chain fatty acid oxidation disorders and current management strategies.
evidence.snippet:
In recent years, the 
use of medium, odd-chain fatty acids, such as triheptanoin, have been studied as 
a treatment of LC-FAODs due to its anaplerotic properties.

evidence.reference_title: Nutritional Management of Patients with Fatty Acid Oxidation Disorders.
evidence.snippet:
Trihepatnoin is a new therapeutic option with a 
good safety and efficacy profile.
